title: Wall structure with three dimensional interface between metal and ceramic matrix composite portions
abstract: There is provided a component (100) a ceramic matrix composite portion (58) having a three dimensional surface (68). The ceramic matrix composite portion (58) further includes a ceramic matrix reinforced with a three-dimensional ceramic fiber material (83). In addition, the component (100) includes a metal portion (60) comprising a three dimensional surface (72) that interfaces with the three dimensional surface (68) of the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) to enable thermal and mechanical load transfer between the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) and the metal portion (60).
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What we claim is: 1. A component (100) comprising: a ceramic matrix composite portion (58) having a three dimensional surface (68), the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) further comprising a ceramic matrix reinforced with a three-dimensional ceramic fiber material (83); and a metal portion (60) comprising a three dimensional surface (72) that interfaces with the three dimensional surface (68) of the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) to enable thermal and mechanical load transfer between the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) and the metal portion (60). 2. The component of claim 1 , wherein the three dimensional surface (72) of the metal portion (60) comprises a metal lattice structure (74). 3. The component of claim 2, wherein the metal lattice structure (58) comprises an additively manufactured metal lattice structure. 4. The component of claim 2, wherein the metal lattice structure (74) comprises a plurality of intersecting segments (75) defining open spaces (76) therebetween, the open spaces (76) having a predetermined shape. 5. The component of claim 4, wherein the lattice portion (74) comprises a grid structure (77). 6. The component of claim 1 , wherein the three-dimensional surface (72) of the metal portion (60) comprises solid metal (65). 7. The component of claims 1 to 6, wherein the metal portion (60) comprises a plurality of radially extending near wall cooling channels (98) there through. 8. The component of claim 1 , wherein three-dimensional fiber material (83) comprises a three dimensional woven fiber material. 9. The component of claim 1 , wherein the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) comprises a second three dimensional surface (86), and wherein the component (100) further comprises a thermal barrier coating (92) on the ceramic matrix composite portion (58), the thermal barrier coating (92) having a three-dimensional surface (90) that interfaces with the second three-dimensional surface (86). 10. The component of claim 1 , wherein the component comprises an airfoil (32), and wherein the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) comprises an outer wall (36) of the airfoil (32). 11. The component of claims 1 to 10, wherein the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) comprises an oxide-oxide matrix material having oxide fibers disposed within an oxide matrix. 12. A method for manufacturing a component (100) comprising: providing a metal portion (60) comprising a three dimensional surface (72); providing a ceramic matrix composite portion (58) having a three-dimensional surface (68), the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) further comprising a ceramic matrix reinforced with a three-dimensional ceramic fiber material (83); and interfacing the three dimensional surface (68) of the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) with the three dimensional surface (72) of the metal portion (58) to enable thermal and mechanical load transfer between the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) and the metal portion (60). 13. The process of claim 12, wherein the three dimensional surface (72) of the metal portion (60) comprises a metal lattice structure (74). 14. The process of claim 13, wherein the metal lattice structure (74) is additively manufactured on the metal portion (60). 15. The process of claim 13, wherein the metal lattice structure (74) comprises a plurality of intersecting segments (75) defining open spaces (76) therebetween, the open spaces (76) having a predetermined shape. 16. The process of claim 13, wherein the three-dimensional surface (72) of the metal portion (58) comprises solid metal (65). 17. The process of claim 12, wherein three dimensional fiber material (83) comprises a three dimensional woven fiber material. 18. The process of claim 12, wherein the ceramic matrix composite portion (58) comprises a second three dimensional surface (86), and further comprising depositing a thermal barrier coating (92) on the ceramic matrix composite portion (58), the thermal barrier coating (92) having a three-dimensional surface (90) that interfaces with the second three dimensional surface (86) of the ceramic matrix composite portion (58). 19. The process of claims 12 to 18, wherein the metal portion (60) comprises a plurality of radially extending near wall cooling channels (98) there through.
